recipe.title: Buttered Egg Toast
steps: Toast the bread and spread butter while warm.
Fry or scramble the eggs to your preference.
Place the eggs on top of the buttered toast.
Season with salt and pepper, and serve.
storage_advice: Bread: Store at room temperature in a bread box or paper bag; refrigeration speeds staling. Freeze for longer storage.
Egg: Keep refrigerated in their original carton, pointed end down, away from the fridge door to limit temperature swings.
Butter: Keep refrigerated in its wrapper or a covered dish; can be frozen for up to 6 months.
sustainability_tips: Bread: Stale bread can be turned into croutons, breadcrumbs, or bread pudding instead of being wasted.
Egg: Eggs that are slightly past their best can still be tested with a float test in water before discarding.
Butter: Buy only what you need; butter has a high greenhouse gas footprint per kilogram relative to plant fats.

summary: Buttered Egg Toast makes good use of your available ingredients and comes in at around 360 calories per serving. Use bread first, as they spoil soonest.

recipe.title: Garlic Tomato Pasta
steps: Boil pasta until al dente; reserve a splash of pasta water.
Sauté minced garlic in olive oil until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes and simmer into a light sauce.
Toss in the cooked pasta with reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce.
Season with salt, pepper, and herbs to taste.
storage_advice: Tomato: Store at room temperature away from sunlight until ripe, then refrigerate. Refrigeration before ripening dulls flavor.
sustainability_tips: Tomato: Tomatoes have a high water footprint; use overripe ones in sauces or soups rather than discarding.

summary: Garlic Tomato Pasta makes good use of your available ingredients and comes in at around 450 calories per serving.
